message:
fix(fortify): treat destlen == usize::MAX as unknown buffer size in all _chk functions

glibc passes destlen == (size_t)-1 when __builtin_object_size cannot
determine the destination buffer size at compile time. With the previous
checks, this sentinel value caused spurious __chk_fail aborts because
any non-trivial len > usize::MAX is always false, but the comparison
len > destlen would succeed for practical buffer sizes since destlen
appears enormous but is actually "unknown".

Add destlen != usize::MAX guards to all 30+ fortify _chk functions:
__memcpy_chk, __memmove_chk, __memset_chk, __explicit_bzero_chk,
__strcpy_chk, __strncpy_chk, __strcat_chk, __strncat_chk,
__stpcpy_chk, __stpncpy_chk, __snprintf_chk, __vsnprintf_chk,
__fgets_chk, __fgets_unlocked_chk, __fread_chk, __fread_unlocked_chk,
__read_chk, __pread_chk, __pread64_chk, __recv_chk, __recvfrom_chk,
__getcwd_chk, __readlink_chk, __readlinkat_chk, __gethostname_chk,
__getdomainname_chk, __confstr_chk, __wcscpy_chk, __wcsncpy_chk,
__wcscat_chk, __wcsncat_chk, __wmemcpy_chk, __wmemmove_chk,
__wmemset_chk, __swprintf_chk, __vswprintf_chk, __fgetws_chk,
__fgetws_unlocked_chk, __mbstowcs_chk, __wcstombs_chk,
__mbsrtowcs_chk, __wcsrtombs_chk, __mbsnrtowcs_chk, __wcsnrtombs_chk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
